Mechanization and Automation for Specialty Crops, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–334, title VII, § 7610132 Stat. 4830Dec. 20, 2018“(a).—In GeneralNot later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Secretary [of Agriculture] shall conduct a review of the programs of the Department of Agriculture that affect the production or processing of specialty crops.Dec. 20, 2018“(b).—RequirementsThe review under subsection (a) shall identify—“(1)programs that currently are, or previously have been, effectively used to accelerate the development and use of automation or mechanization in the production or processing of specialty crops; and“(2)programs that may be more effectively used to accelerate the development and use of automation or mechanization in the production or processing of specialty crops.“(c).—StrategyWith respect to programs identified under subsection (b), the Secretary shall develop and implement a strategy to accelerate the development and use of automation and mechanization in the production or processing of specialty crops.”

Emergency Citrus Disease Research and Development Trust Fund, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–334, title XII, § 12605132 Stat. 5006Pub. L. 118–22, div. B, title I, § 102(d)(8)(D)137 Stat. 118Pub. L. 119–21, title I, § 10607(f)139 Stat. 111Dec. 20, 2018Nov. 17, 2023July 4, 2025“(a).—Definition of CitrusIn this section, the term ‘citrus’ means edible fruit of the family Rutaceae, including any hybrid of that fruit and any product of that hybrid that is produced for commercial purposes in the United States.“(b).—Establishment of Trust FundThere is established in the Treasury of the United States a trust fund, to be known as the Emergency Citrus Disease Research and Development Trust Fund (referred to in this section as the ‘Citrus Trust Fund’), consisting of such amounts as shall be transferred to the Citrus Trust Fund pursuant to subsection (d).“(c).—Use of FundFrom amounts in the Citrus Trust Fund, the Secretary shall, beginning in fiscal year 2019, carry out the Emergency Citrus Disease Research and Extension Program in section 412(j) of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 ().7 U.S.C. 7632(j)“(d).—FundingOf the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall transfer to the Citrus Trust Fund $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2031, to remain available until expended.”

children.heading: Citrus disease subcommittee
children.label: 1 Citrus disease subcommittee
children.text: The term “citrus disease subcommittee” means the subcommittee established under .section 3123a(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Initiative
children.label: 2 Initiative
children.text: The term “Initiative” means the specialty crop research and extension initiative established by subsection (b).

children.heading: Specialty crop
children.label: 3 Specialty crop
children.text: The term “specialty crop” has the meaning given that term in section 3 of the Specialty Crops Competitiveness Act of 2004 ( note; ).7 U.S.C. 1621Public Law 108–465

children.heading: Specialty crops committee
children.label: 4 Specialty crops committee
children.text: The term “specialty crops committee” means the committee established under .section 3123a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: product, taste, quality, and appearance;

children.label: B
children.text: size-controlling rootstock systems for perennial crops;

children.label: C
children.text: environmental responses and tolerances;

children.label: D
children.text: nutrient management, including plant nutrient uptake efficiency;

children.label: E
children.text: pest and disease management, including resistance to pests and diseases resulting in reduced application management strategies; and

children.label: F
children.text: enhanced phytonutrient content;

children.label: A
children.text: threats to specialty crop pollinators;

children.label: B
children.text: emerging and invasive species; and

children.label: C
children.text: a more effective understanding and utilization of existing natural enemy complexes;

children.label: A
children.text: to improve production efficiency, handling and processing, productivity, and profitability over the long term (including specialty crop policy and marketing); and

children.label: i
children.text: the soil rhizosphere microbiome;

children.label: ii
children.text: pesticide application systems and certified drift-reduction technologies; and

children.label: iii
children.text: systems to improve and extend the storage life of specialty crops;

children.label: A
children.text: mechanization and automation of labor-intensive tasks in production and processing;

children.label: B
children.text: technologies that delay or inhibit ripening;

children.label: C
children.text: decision support systems driven by phenology and environmental factors;

children.label: D
children.text: improved monitoring systems for agricultural pests; and

children.label: E
children.text: effective systems for preharvest and postharvest management of quarantine pests; and
methods to prevent, detect, monitor, control, and respond to potential food safety hazards in the production and processing of specialty crops, including fresh produce.
Federal agencies;
national laboratories;
colleges and universities;
research institutions and organizations;
private organizations or corporations;
State agricultural experiment stations;
individuals; or
groups consisting of 2 or more entities described in paragraphs (1) through (7).
a scientific peer review conducted by a panel of subject matter experts from Federal agencies, non-Federal entities, and the specialty crop industry; and
a review and ranking for merit, relevance, and impact conducted by a panel of specialty crop industry representatives for the specific specialty crop.
in the first fiscal year in which that consultation occurs, any recommendations for conducting such reviews in such fiscal year; and

children.label: A
children.text: an assessment of the procedures and objectives used by the Secretary for such reviews in the previous fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: any recommendations for such reviews for the current fiscal year; and

children.label: C
children.text: any comments on grants awarded under subsection (d) during the previous fiscal year.
the results of the consultations with the specialty crops committee (and subcommittees thereof) conducted under subsection (e) of this section and subsection (g) of ;section 3123a of this title
the specialty crops committee’s (and subcommittees thereof) recommendations, if any, provided to the Secretary during such consultations; and
the specialty crops committee’s (and subcommittees thereof) review of the grants awarded under subsection (d) and (j), as applicable, in the previous fiscal year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to grants awarded under this section, the Secretary shall seek and accept proposals for grants.

children.heading: Term
children.label: 2 Term
children.text: The term of a grant under this section may not exceed 10 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An entity receiving a grant under this section shall provide non-Federal matching funds (including funds from an agricultural commodity promotion, research, and information program) equal to not less than the amount of the grant.

children.heading: In-kind support
children.label: B In-kind support
children.text: Non-Federal matching funds described in subparagraph (A) may include in-kind support.

children.heading: Other conditions
children.label: 4 Other conditions
children.text: The Secretary may set such other conditions on the award of a grant under the Initiative as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
are multistate, multi-institutional, or multidisciplinary; and
include explicit mechanisms to communicate results to producers and the public.

children.heading: Buildings and facilities
children.label: i Buildings and facilities
children.text: Funds made available under this section shall not be used for the construction of a new building or facility or the acquisition, expansion, remodeling, or alteration of an existing building or facility (including site grading and improvement, and architect fees).

children.heading: Emergency citrus disease research and extension program
children.label: j Emergency citrus disease research and extension program
A
children.text: to conduct scientific research and extension activities, technical assistance, and development activities to combat citrus diseases and pests, both domestic and invasive, which pose imminent harm to the United States citrus production and threaten the future viability of the citrus industry, including and the Asian Citrus Psyllid; andhuanglongbing

children.label: i
children.text: the emergency citrus disease research and extension program; or

children.label: ii
children.text: other research and extension projects intended to solve problems caused by citrus production diseases and invasive pests.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 2 Priority
children.text: In awarding grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall give priority to grants that address the research and extension priorities established pursuant to subsection (g)(4) of .section 3123a of this title

children.label: A
children.text: seek input from Federal and State agencies and other entities involved in citrus disease response; and

children.label: B
children.text: take into account other public and private citrus-related research and extension projects and the funding for such projects.

children.heading: Nonduplication
children.label: 4 Nonduplication
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that funds made available to carry out the emergency citrus disease research and extension activities under this subsection shall be in addition to and not supplant funds made available to carry out other citrus disease activities carried out by the Department of Agriculture in consultation with State agencies.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: In addition to the amounts reserved under subsection (k)(1)(C), there are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection, $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018.

children.heading: Citrus
children.label: A Citrus
children.text: The term “citrus” means edible fruit of the family Rutaceae, including any hybrid of such fruits and products of such hybrids that are produced for commercial purposes in the United States.

children.heading: Citrus producer
children.label: B Citrus producer
children.text: The term “citrus producer” means any person that is engaged in the domestic production and commercial sale of citrus in the United States.

children.heading: Emergency citrus disease research and extension program
children.label: C Emergency citrus disease research and extension program
children.text: The term “emergency citrus disease research and extension program” means the emergency citrus research and extension grant program established under this subsection.

children.heading: Fiscal years 2008 through 2012
children.label: A Fiscal years 2008 through 2012
children.text: Of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall make available to carry out this section $30,000,000 for fiscal year 2008 and $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2012, from which activities under each of paragraphs (1) through (5) of subsection (b) shall be allocated not less than 10 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2025; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $175,000,000 for fiscal year 2026 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.heading: Reservation
children.label: C Reservation
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2014 through 2018, the Secretary shall reserve not less than $25,000,000 of the funds made available under subparagraph (B) to carry out the program established under subsection (j).

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: D Availability of funds
children.text: Funds reserved under subparagraph (C) shall remain available and reserved for the purpose described in such subparagraph until expended.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations for fiscal years 2014 through 2023
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations for fiscal years 2014 through 2023
children.text: In addition to funds made available under paragraph (1), there is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $100,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.

children.heading: Fiscal year 2013
children.label: 3 Fiscal year 2013
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $100,000,000 for fiscal year 2013.

children.heading: Transfer
children.label: 4 Transfer
children.text: Of the funds made available to the Secretary under paragraph (1) for fiscal year 2008 and authorized for use for payment of administrative expenses under , the Secretary shall transfer, upon the date of enactment of this section, $200,000 to the Office of Prevention, Pesticides, and Toxic Substances of the Environmental Protection Agency for use in conducting a meta-analysis relating to methyl bromide.section 3315(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 5 Availability
children.text: Funds made available pursuant to this subsection for a fiscal year shall remain available until expended to pay for obligations incurred in that fiscal year.
